@graph.name: TOGAF Architecture Development Method Ontology
@graph.description: Comprehensive connected ontology for TOGAF ADM with full relationship semantics covering phases, artifacts, building blocks, requirements, and governance

@graph.rdfs:label: ADM Phase
@graph.rdfs:comment: A phase in the TOGAF Architecture Development Method cycle

@graph.rdfs:label: Architecture Domain
@graph.rdfs:comment: One of the four TOGAF architecture domains

@graph.rdfs:label: Architecture Artifact
@graph.rdfs:comment: A work product produced during architecture development

@graph.rdfs:label: Catalog
@graph.rdfs:comment: A list of building blocks of a specific type

@graph.rdfs:label: Matrix
@graph.rdfs:comment: A grid showing relationships between building blocks

@graph.rdfs:label: Diagram
@graph.rdfs:comment: A visual representation of building blocks and relationships

@graph.rdfs:label: Deliverable
@graph.rdfs:comment: A contractually specified work product

@graph.rdfs:label: Building Block
@graph.rdfs:comment: A potentially reusable component of enterprise capability

@graph.rdfs:label: Architecture Building Block (ABB)
@graph.rdfs:comment: A component of the architecture model describing required capability

@graph.rdfs:label: Solution Building Block (SBB)
@graph.rdfs:comment: A component that implements an Architecture Building Block

@graph.rdfs:label: Architecture Requirement
@graph.rdfs:comment: A statement of need that must be met by the architecture

@graph.rdfs:label: Architecture Principle
@graph.rdfs:comment: A general rule and guideline intended to be enduring

@graph.rdfs:label: Architecture Constraint
@graph.rdfs:comment: An external factor that limits solution options

@graph.rdfs:label: Architecture Gap
@graph.rdfs:comment: Difference between baseline and target architecture

@graph.rdfs:label: Architecture Risk
@graph.rdfs:comment: A potential issue that may impact architecture success

@graph.rdfs:label: Stakeholder
@graph.rdfs:comment: An individual, team or organization with interests in the architecture

@graph.rdfs:label: Stakeholder Concern
@graph.rdfs:comment: An interest or requirement of a stakeholder

@graph.rdfs:label: Architecture Viewpoint
@graph.rdfs:comment: A specification of conventions for constructing and using a view

@graph.rdfs:label: Architecture View
@graph.rdfs:comment: A representation of a system from a specific viewpoint

@graph.rdfs:label: Transition Architecture
@graph.rdfs:comment: An intermediate architecture state between baseline and target

@graph.rdfs:label: Work Package
@graph.rdfs:comment: A set of activities and deliverables for implementation

@graph.rdfs:label: Architecture Contract
@graph.rdfs:comment: A joint agreement on deliverables, quality and fitness-for-purpose

@graph.rdfs:label: Compliance Assessment
@graph.rdfs:comment: Assessment of implementation conformance to architecture

@graph.rdfs:label: Capability
@graph.rdfs:comment: An ability that an organization possesses

@graph.rdfs:label: Capability Increment
@graph.rdfs:comment: A discrete portion of capability delivered through projects

@graph.name: Business Architecture
@graph.description: The business strategy, governance, organization, and key business processes

@graph.name: Data Architecture
@graph.description: The structure of logical and physical data assets and data management resources

@graph.name: Application Architecture
@graph.description: A blueprint for individual applications, their interactions, and relationships to business processes

@graph.name: Technology Architecture
@graph.description: The logical software and hardware capabilities required to support business, data and application services

@graph.name: Preliminary Phase
@graph.description: Prepare the organization for successful TOGAF architecture projects
@graph.togaf:objective: Determine the Architecture Capability desired by the organization

@graph.name: Phase A: Architecture Vision
@graph.description: Develop a high-level aspirational vision of the capabilities and business value
@graph.togaf:objective: Obtain approval for a Statement of Architecture Work

@graph.name: Phase B: Business Architecture
@graph.description: Develop the Business Architecture to support the agreed Architecture Vision
@graph.togaf:objective: Develop the Target Business Architecture describing product and service strategy

@graph.name: Phase C: Information Systems Architectures
@graph.description: Develop the Data and Application Architectures
@graph.togaf:objective: Develop Target Data and Application Architectures enabling the Business Architecture

@graph.name: Phase D: Technology Architecture
@graph.description: Develop the Technology Architecture to enable logical and physical application and data components
@graph.togaf:objective: Develop Target Technology Architecture enabling Application and Data Architectures

@graph.name: Phase E: Opportunities and Solutions
@graph.description: Generate the initial complete version of the Architecture Roadmap
@graph.togaf:objective: Generate the Implementation and Migration Strategy

@graph.name: Phase F: Migration Planning
@graph.description: Finalize the Architecture Roadmap and Implementation and Migration Plan
@graph.togaf:objective: Finalize detailed Implementation and Migration Plan

@graph.name: Phase G: Implementation Governance
@graph.description: Ensure conformance with the Target Architecture by implementation projects

@graph.name: Phase H: Architecture Change Management
@graph.description: Establish and support implemented architecture and manage change
@graph.togaf:objective: Ensure architecture lifecycle is maintained and change is managed

@graph.name: Requirements Management
@graph.description: Manage architecture requirements throughout the ADM cycle
@graph.togaf:objective: Ensure requirements are available for each phase of ADM

@graph.name: Consolidated Gaps, Solutions, and Dependencies Matrix

@graph.rdfs:label: precedes
@graph.rdfs:comment: Phase A precedes Phase B in the ADM cycle

@graph.rdfs:label: follows
@graph.rdfs:comment: Inverse of precedes - Phase B follows Phase A

@graph.rdfs:label: produces
@graph.rdfs:comment: Phase produces deliverable/artifact as output

@graph.rdfs:label: consumes
@graph.rdfs:comment: Phase consumes deliverable/artifact as input

@graph.rdfs:label: governs
@graph.rdfs:comment: Entity provides governance over another

@graph.rdfs:label: validates
@graph.rdfs:comment: Entity validates or confirms another

@graph.rdfs:label: informs
@graph.rdfs:comment: Entity provides information to another

@graph.rdfs:label: refines
@graph.rdfs:comment: Entity provides more detail to another

@graph.rdfs:label: transforms
@graph.rdfs:comment: Entity transforms baseline to target state

@graph.rdfs:label: derives
@graph.rdfs:comment: Entity is derived from source entities

@graph.rdfs:label: traces
@graph.rdfs:comment: Entity traces to requirements or other artifacts

@graph.rdfs:label: realizes
@graph.rdfs:comment: Entity realizes or implements another

@graph.rdfs:label: maps
@graph.rdfs:comment: Matrix or artifact maps relationships between catalogs

@graph.rdfs:label: visualizes
@graph.rdfs:comment: Diagram visualizes content from catalog or deliverable

@graph.rdfs:label: contains
@graph.rdfs:comment: Entity contains other entities

@graph.rdfs:label: constrains
@graph.rdfs:comment: Entity constrains options for another

@graph.rdfs:label: decomposes
@graph.rdfs:comment: Entity decomposes another into finer granularity

@graph.rdfs:label: triggers
@graph.rdfs:comment: Entity triggers initiation of another

@graph.rdfs:label: impacts
@graph.rdfs:comment: Entity has impact on another

@graph.rdfs:label: addresses
@graph.rdfs:comment: Phase or artifact addresses a concern

@graph.rdfs:label: supports
@graph.rdfs:comment: Entity supports another

@graph.rdfs:label: associated with
@graph.rdfs:comment: Entity is associated with another (non-directional)

@graph.rdfs:label: phase code
@graph.rdfs:comment: Standard TOGAF phase identifier (Preliminary, A-H, RM)

@graph.rdfs:label: objective
@graph.rdfs:comment: Primary objective of the ADM phase
